title: Change the 'View' order on a board
excerpt: The details below were posted in 2021 by someone. I have copied and pasted to here as he was asking for a new feature. Why is this not an option? Crazy that when we create a new view it just goes to the end and there is …

posts.author: MaxLogic_Lisa (Lisa Armstrong)
posts.cookedHtml:
The details below were posted in 2021 by someone. I have copied and pasted to here as he was asking for a new feature. Why is this not an option? Crazy that when we create a new view it just goes to the end and there is no way of sorting them.

Has this happened. ?? I cannot find a way to do this on a board level and not user level.

“Currently boards don’t allow custom views to be rearranged in a custom order. We use views to filter the items in unique ways and want to prioritize certain ones by putting them in the front, and putting less important ones at the end. I can mark a view as a favorite so it moves closer to the front, but even that doesn’t allow me to rearrange ordering without refavoriting them all. Would love it if it had drag and drop capability like with item ordering as well as a way to lock/unlock the arrangement to prevent accidental changes.”

Thank you
Lisa

posts.author: Macainian (Rob Hostetler)
posts.cookedHtml: I absolutely agree and am still shocked that here it is almost a year later from your post and it has not been implemented. I understand how things can be difficult to implement if certain structure is already in place that has boxed Monday into not being able to easy reorder these, but even if that is the case, this has to be one of the most basic and highly requested features that Monday has, so why hasn’t it been addressed yet? Monday, please bump this basic feature up in priority.

posts.author: Macainian (Rob Hostetler)
posts.cookedHtml: Does anyone know if there is an app/integration that can do this?

posts.author: erozencwaig (Eric Rozencwaig)
posts.cookedHtml: any updates to this request?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,

We appreciate that there is demand for this feature.

We are excited to announce that this feature is currently under consideration, however soon to be added to our roadmap! We will be updating the features development status in the coming weeks, so please keep an eye on the existing feature request here

posts.author: EadW (Edwin)
posts.cookedHtml:
This was literally the first thing I noticed that when you create views, that you would want to sort or rearrange them based on your needs, and Monday does not allow it and instead forces you to EDIT all existing tabs to make your order possible…

Shocked it takes 1,5 year to pick up such basic feature.

Unless there is some deep technical reason why it cannot be done in a quick sprint, makes you wonder how (or even IF) Monday is using their software internally …

posts.author: MaxLogic_Lisa (Lisa Armstrong)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello Bianca. Would really love an update on this. As you can see in August you asked me to track on another feature request called ‘Ability to Reorder Views’ and still nothing. You did mention there would be an update in the coming weeks. How many weeks? Please can you advise us all. Best regards, Lisa

posts.author: jcellitti (Jessica Cellitti)
posts.cookedHtml:
I can’t believe this is not an option! All of the WONDERFUL things I can do with my board and I can’t drag the views around.

I just spent 20 minutes searching for this ability because I am a Monday.com newbie optimizing an old board for my team from 2019 and am in shock!

posts.author: MaxLogic_Lisa (Lisa Armstrong)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Jessica. I have been saying this for a year!! A lot of other Monday users also agree. It is really frustrating that the tab views are just static and the only way to reorder is to make them default and then make another default and so on till they are in the order that you want. Ridiculous!

posts.author: CNunez (Christianne Nunez)
posts.cookedHtml: It is my duty to add myself to the frustrated group here. I remember I sent my feedback on this long time ago (can’t remember when), and was something simpler such as “can we re-arrange in alphabetical order at least?”… still waiting.

posts.author: tfVu8 (Your full name)
posts.cookedHtml: Another simple feature neglected by the Monday.com team. What’s new? Perhaps we will see the feature in 2025. I’ll be gone by then.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
Elevate 2023 announced that board view reordering, as well as board view permissions is coming in 2024.

Watching Elevate 2023’s 2024 product roadmap video is worthwhile.

Elevate 2023 by monday.com

Watch - Elevate 2023 by monday.com

Get access to watch all of the sessions from Elevate '23 including keynote sessions, our product roadmap, and more!

posts.author: tfVu8 (Your full name)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks, Cody. I am curious, why does it take three years?

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: Not a clue, I’m just a monday partner not monday employee. I’d say the #1 thing was the mondayDB project which was needed to allow working with larger data sets. Lifting the 10,000 item limit (going to 100,000 soon) is probably the biggest thing in early 2024. I consider that 10K limit to be a fundamental problem with the platform that was a roadblock for a lot of organizations. So those underlying “invisible” changes needed to be done first, because all the existing code had to be adapted to them first - before then making more changes (unless you want to cause instability and lots of bugs)

posts.author: tfVu8 (Your full name)
posts.cookedHtml: That makes sense. Thanks for providing the explanation, Cody, I appreciate it.

posts.author: toddwh (Todd White)
posts.cookedHtml: Still not implemented?? This seems like something so obvious that it would never occur to me that it doesn’t exist.

posts.author: GiannisKoukounas (Giannis Koukounas)
posts.cookedHtml: Very hopeful that we are going to get this relatively soon, I believe this was an infrastructure issue, and now that we have gotten/are getting new mondayDB updates this should also be on the roadmap.

posts.author: andrewst (Tyler Andrews)
posts.cookedHtml: This will be a nice feature once implemented!

posts.author: tomthack13 (Tom Thackeray)
posts.cookedHtml: please please please get this implemented soon!

posts.author: kennyosborn (Kenny Osborn)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, please! We need this soon.

posts.author: MichaelGiovanis (Michael Giovanis)
posts.cookedHtml: Can’t Wait-This is Needed!!

posts.author: chens1 (chen)
posts.cookedHtml:
If anyone needs a hack for this, and doesn’t want to wait for the feature before reordering the views, you can follow these steps:
Supposed you have the following views:
Main Table, View A, View B, View C, View D
and you want the view to be in the order: Main, A, D, B, C
You can do this:
duplicate view B
duplicate view C
[Current state: M, A, B, C, D, B2, C2]
delete view B
delete view C
[Current state: M, A, D, B2, C2]
rename “duplicate of B” to B
rename “duplicate of C” to C
[Final state: M, A, D, B, C]

posts.author: TFaust (Timo Faust)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any updates on this?

In Project Management I use a lot of different views and honestly sorting them via workaround by duplicating and deleting is just not a time sink I can justify.

It was on the roadmap and is such a basic quality of life feature.

Instead of jumping on the AI hype, Monday should cover the basics more, especially with all the system issues of lately.

I get why the focus is on futureproofing Monday or promoting it in this AI battle, but to futureproof some concepts need to be set in the present and not in the past

posts.author: shayan.moussawi (Shayan Moussawi)
posts.cookedHtml:
They addressed it here

Ability to Reorder Views Feature requests
Update from the product team: The final touches are being put on this feature and you will very soon be able to re-order views! The Alpha release of the feature is planned to start in the coming weeks and then it will start to be gradually released. We are planing for you be able to reorder the views in any way you want before the end of 2024.

It is coming as an alpha release in the coming weeks and there will be a full release by the end of the year.

posts.author: TFaust (Timo Faust)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the info

posts.author: awilding (Abner Wilding)
posts.cookedHtml: Awesome! Thank you, team! Can hardly wait. This will be a game changer now we’re using ‘Group By’ more often. Thanks again.

posts.author: venkat_reniguntla (Venkat)
posts.cookedHtml: Business perspective very helpful to implement the feature to allow users reorder views using drag and drop feature. Any business wants that business perspective important views at the start and less important views at the end.

posts.author: kamila (Kamila Hughes)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1
It should have been part of the basic feature set from the get-go.

posts.author: Robert_Bogner (Robert Bogner)
posts.cookedHtml: The above post from December 31, 2023 says that this feature will come in 2024. There are only a few weeks left until the end of the year - is it already available? (I can’t find it.)

posts.author: WLane (Wally Lane)
posts.cookedHtml: Looks like this might be getting close to being released. It says by the end of 2024. Follow this one for updates: Ability to Reorder Views - Feature requests - monday Community Forum
